themes.reasoning_notes: Cheap Aura that repeatedly draws cards when the enchanted opposing creature taps; its main deck role is sustained card advantage.
It is specifically an Aura, though the subtype matters less than the repeatable draw trigger in gameplay.
Best on creatures likely to attack or tap, passively rewarding the controller in slower tempo-oriented play.
Aura on an opposing creature that can draw repeatedly whenever that creature taps; its clearest deck role is ongoing card advantage.
It is specifically an Aura, so it can contribute to Aura-count synergies even though the draw trigger is the main reason to play it.
